title: Civil Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft preliminary site layouts, run routine structural load checks, and generate permit paperwork, but stamped drawings, public-safety liability, and site-specific judgment remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: BIM-integrated AI plug-ins for clash detection and quantity takeoffs are credible and improving, but full autonomous civil design is far from mature.
rationales.adoption: Large engineering firms use AI-assisted BIM and takeoff tools; most municipal and small-firm civil engineering work still runs on traditional workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-check drawings for code compliance and flag clashes across disciplines, but site inspection, stakeholder negotiation, and PE stamping are not agent-reachable.

tasksAtRisk: Preliminary site layout and grading drafts
Routine structural load and code-compliance checks
Quantity takeoffs and permit application paperwork
tasksGrowing: Site inspection and field condition assessment
Public-safety liability sign-off on stamped drawings
Stakeholder negotiation on public infrastructure projects
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2051, p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now handles quantity takeoffs and BIM clash checks, but stamped drawings still carry an engineer's legal liability. Civil engineers who lean into field inspection and public-project stakeholder work stay ahead of the automation curve.
